name: Kudzaishe Mwaza
bio: I'm a tech enthusiast passionate about the intersection of education, robotics, and artificial intelligence. Currently, I teach STEM subjects to high school students, helping them build strong conceptual foundations and curiosity-driven thinking.

My journey in Mechatronics Engineering sparked a deep interest in how intelligent systems can solve real-world problems — from autonomous robots to AI-driven learning tools. I enjoy bridging complex technical ideas with accessible explanations that empower others to learn and create.
currentFocus: Building cloud architecture and DevOps skills
Reading papers on human-centered AI and educational robotics

researchInterests.title: Educational Robotics
researchInterests.description: Developing accessible robotics platforms for STEM education in underserved communities.

researchInterests.title: AI for Low-Resource and Socially Grounded Applications
researchInterests.description: Building adaptive AI systems that serve diverse communities and environments.

researchInterests.title: Human-Centered AI
researchInterests.description: Building AI systems that enhance human capabilities and promote accessibility.

teaching.title: Physics
teaching.subtitle: Mechanics • Electricity & Magnetism • Waves
teaching.description: Tutored 100+ students; built conceptual foundations with problem-solving drills and exam prep. Integrated real-world contexts and lab-style reasoning to strengthen intuition.

teaching.title: Mathematics
teaching.subtitle: Algebra • Calculus • Problem Solving
teaching.description: Designed individualized plans; emphasized conceptual understanding and exam strategy. Encouraged active learning and mathematical confidence through guided discovery, discussion, and adaptive feedback.

teaching.title: Programming Fundamentals (College Prep)
teaching.subtitle: Programming Fundamentals
teaching.description: Mentored students on Python basics, problem decomposition, and real-world applications. Integrated AI tools (Copilot, Claude, Cursor) to build computational literacy and independence. Delivered free community lessons and supported peer-learning circles.
